channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 126 replies — 504.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 126 • Updated: 2026-04-28 17:10 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62163772
>>62158557
Being a professional is probably the worst. I'm a lawyer and I want to kms. Sure it makes me feel cool but all I do is work and stress. I literally got vitiligo from stress.

62163805
>>62163706
Because wealth isn't mainly determined by intelligence. It depends on what you do with your time, the systems you're in, and whether you're building or owning capital.

62163971
>>62163772
So you envy NEETs?

62164153
>>62158557
Neeting is super comfy but only if your parents are chill or you have a single mother

If your dad is buzzing around all day with negative comments and a scrunched up face you'll suffer

I became a wageslave out of necessity, not out of choice. been waging for over 10 years now and its taken a lot away from me. I don't recognise myself anymore

At least I got a gf and had a lot of sex at one point. Being a slave without getting laid is the worst position to be in

62164168
>>62159910
T. Masturbating 8 times a day

62164405
>>62158557
It's worse to be a NEET with no long term financial plans. All the NEET with no plans at all have totally rekt themselves. They're basically failed normies who have all given up. Life doesn't care if you give up, it still fucks rekts u regardless. Real tism doesn't give up, it just problem solves toward a new path that's more agreeable to their nature.

This is why crypto NEET is the only option. Then you graduate to semi-retired equities oldfag.

You're not Diogenes you dumb failed normie cunts. Save yourself. No one else will.

62164435
>>62164405
i am diogenes though

62164438
>>62164435

62164442
>>62164438
my sides

62164485
Okay site's up. I'd post it but I'm too drunk to log in as an admin now it's too much work to let chuds flood the site overnight
Yare yare daze

62164486
>>62164485
>>62160116
>>62159061
>>62159212
Webdev achieved
https://youtu.be/s64HwyhHlW4

62164494
I've been working remote for 7 years and honestly its like neeting lite. I just look at the bad screen for 8 hours in exchange for a decent salary. And I dont have to commute, its literally 8 hours and thats it.

62164500
>>62158726
>doesnt tripple check his banking account
Stay poor, fag.

62164528
>>62164494
Is this troll post lol

62164654
https://youtu.be/Eiy9vAFcl3U

62164804
>>62158557
lol this entire thread is “having a job is better so I can enjoy some pocket money”
lol yeah go to your shitty job 8 hours a fucking day so you can go to a resto once per week and buy your weed edibles and that’s all you’ll ever get lmao. Yeha nah being a neet is far better just scam your boomers into co signing a loan for day trading. Other than that working is a 100% total waste of time and isn’t going to get you a house and even if you get a house you will lose it shortly after. Because working = dependent and self employment = independence and no wagie has ever experienced true independence bcuz the second they are fired they better go through the cycle again and again and again forever while the rich compound gains while you look for your next cuck postion at shit jobz pathetic

62164812
>>62158557
I was going to say Neet is worse but dealing with people is hell so I'm going to say Wagie. Having money is nice but I rather not splurge than have stupid coworkers and customers take advantage of you because you're trying to keep your job.

62164866
>me doing whatever I want all day because I have no job

62164928
>>62158601
Fact check: most wagies don't have the time or the money to go out and have fun and socialize.

62165024
Both sides in this thread are coping a bit.
NEET feels like freedom at first but for most people it turns into stagnation unless you're extremely self-directed. You lose structure, your sense of time gets weird, and eventually nothing feels that rewarding.
Wagie life sucks in a different way (routine, low autonomy, sometimes humiliation) but it gives you money, exposure to people, and at least some structure.
NEET + no direction = decay
Wagie + no progression = slow trap
NEET + discipline (learning, building, training) = rare but viable
Wagie + skill-building / saving = actually opens options over time
Most people in this thread are arguing about comfort vs discomfort, not outcomes.
Also: if you're the type who does nothing with your free time while working you're not suddenly going to become productive as a NEET. It usually gets worse, not better.
If you care about long-term quality of life, the question is: are you gaining leverage (skills, money, options) or just maintaining your current state?
Everything else is just preference and coping.

62165089
>>62158557
>wagie or a NEET
wagie because you can find yourself surround by negative people for a while
but they're both the worst with a bad mindset desu

62165090
>>62164928
False. Because most people in society are wagies, socializing happens during the same 5pm-2am time frame. It's why being a neet sucks because none of your friends are available as they are working and you can only hangout with them when they are done work which defeats the whole point of you not working.
>>62165024
This.

62165202
>>62165024
Literally ChatGPT written. Also, you aren’t developing fucking skills at your call center job or flipping beef patties. None of those open any options for you and are time sink. Jobs also don’t change your sense of time, all a job does is make you adhere to a schedule and some schedules are fucked up so it’s the same as NEETING. The wealth is concentrated at the top and most of the rich today inherited wealth they did NOT work for it AT ALL. Get that thru your fucking head. You are labor and no laborer ends up like Trump or Musk. Most labor ends up back broken and tossed aside later in life. 50% of all baby boomers are broke and destitute that’s proof enough waging isn’t the correct path

62165417
>>62158557
Waging is worse. The problem with waging is that most jobs are full time with a pitiful allotment of days off.
Like seriously, I get 4 weeks of personal leave a year. That amounts to a couple of 2 week holidays in a year, just fucking nothing. Its such a small amount of time I cant even stand to use it because im psychologically cucked knowing im going to be counting down those days before i have to get back to work again. And then when i get back to work, I will have fewer days left in the bank and the despair will set in further.
Being a neet was being a loser with no hope but at least you can put yourself in a state of mild contentment through trivial entertainment.
Being a wagie is a state of joylessness, boredom and stress. And then you're so time poor you cant enjoy anything in between so the money is irrelevant.
Waging is below me as a human being. I can work, I can feel fulfilled overcoming challenges and building towards things. But i can not, and will not hold up the merits of doing a constant stream of unending tasks 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. There is not a life for a conscious human.

62165497
>>62158557
Wagie. Dealing with normgroids sucks donkey penis.

62165528
The unfortunate truth is that being a NEET is basically the ultimate way of fucking yourself over, unless big inheritance is guaranteed. There's no way of putting into words how fucked a ~30 year old NEET is whose parents are getting up there in age and aren't wealthy. Even if you were in your early twenties, if you've been a NEET for even just a few years you have fucked yourself over massively.

It might not seem that way when you're sitting in your comfy room with electricity and food and bills getting paid for by someone else, but one day, that someone else will be gone. The lights will dim, that person won't be there to give you food or buy you things anymore, and there will be silence. And you'll be alone. And all those years you spent thinking "haha, those wagies are such cucks!" and "idc what happens to me I'll just rope doomer patrick bateman perfect girl edit slowed extended" won't have meant anything. Reality will hit you like the Malaysian Airlines flight 370 Boeing 777 hit the Indian Ocean doing a full speed nosedive. You will realize that you have lived over 1/4th of your life and you haven't begun a career and you have nothing to show for yourself, meanwhile young twenty something are working towards their masters degrees or already working salary jobs. And you'll realize something absolutely catastrophic: you CAN'T rope. You can't do it. There really is no escape, you are only now realizing that you can't bring yourself to do it. You don't have to die to go to hell, you just have to live your life as a NEET.

62165968
>>62165528
>tl:dr wageoid seething
Kys retard.

62166001
>>62165528
>muh show for yourself normoid perspective
Kek. That doesn't matter. The big problem is that there's only so much good anime to watch and games to play. You run out of quality entertainment VERY quickly, and you'll start losing your mind playing MOBA trash or watching moeshit, or being a prominent member of a niche online community, and your days will be filled with nostalgia about enjoying things from years and years ago, now that's a fate worse than death.

62166058
what if the neet is a wagie? he’s just paid a very low wage by society to keep to himself.

62166183
>>62158557
wagie. if your a NEET you can be either a bum or retired but if you're a wagie the majority of your day belongs to making someone else rich

item.title: Proof of Work BTFO: Litecoin Bug Allowed Double Spend and Had A Chain Reorg — 38 replies — 33.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Proof of Work BTFO: Litecoin Bug Allowed Double Spend and Had A Chain Reorg

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:05 UTC

OP

A Litecoin bug found today allowed double spend and led to miners having to reorg multiple blocks. PoW is a failed consensus model. Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it, and that those mining will not continue to sell the network out, with Epstein and co. having already permanently neutered it as spendable medium-of-exchange money to transform it into the random evolution of "store of value," controlling the majority of the core devs across a majority of code build and corporatists still controlling a majority of mining. Bitcoin has had a full chain reorg and Nakamoto probabilistic fork choice algo does not give full settlement for this reason. The best incentive is no incentive on networks which are as secure at 1 dollar as they are at 1000 and this is the only model institutions will meaningfully build actual RWA etc. on.
https://www.theblock.co/post/398892/litecoin-rewrites-three-hours-of-history-to-undo-its-first-major-privacy-layer-exploit

Early replies (first 3)

62156962
>>62156949
I am holding eth too.

62156967
>>62156949

62156991
>>62156967
>.gif
dub- dub- dub-
hi! my name is
>what?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

hi! my name is
>ha?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

hi! my name is
>what?
hi! my name is
>who?
chic-chicka-slim shady

excuse me!
can I have attention of the class
for one second

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62157252
>>62156949
>Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it
I don't own any crypto, but this describes a lot of very important things. Like a physical vault is like that. If someone spends more money than what's used to build/guard the vault, they'll get into the vault.

62157402
>>62157252
Definitely, but this adds a MUCH easier anonymity and custodians are counterparties working on reputation. If JP Morgan vaults get sold out and are uninsured they lose a business worth WAY more than their physical holdings and are personally and institutionally exposed. When proof of work networks are exploited or coopted, there is just a layer of abstraction or investment losers unhappy when the network majority votes to evolve in a way that SCREWS retail and sells out defi values (as has already happened, requiring centralization into super shitty, TURBO-centralized L2s 5o be remotely viable while still being 100x more expensive to use than other networks which scale at L1 with no concentrated risk faster). Bank heists are possible but harder and insured against and much more difficult. Blockchain tech is for sure the future, but PoW is it's proto, beta test origination.

62157467
Litecoin was a coin really loved by 4channellers. Did all the litecoin enjoyers leave?

62157499
>>62156949
>PoW is a failed consensus model
>Bitcoin has had a full chain reorg
>Nakamoto probabilistic fork choice algo does not give full settlement
All of this is actual made up fake bullshit, you are literally saying nothing at all. What happened today with Litecoin has nothing to do with its consensus algorithm. You are a giant piece of shit and I sincerely hope you die soon. My schizo theory is that this is somehow related to Calude's Mythos AI because this shit is really good at finding zero day exploits and from what I can gather this was one.

62157670
>>62157499
the irony is that there are countless zero days because modern systems are built like shit. but hackers don't exploit them because it's pointless and retarded to do it. dunning kruger ai retards don't get that

62157685
Not the win OP thinks it is. Litecoin didn't get a malicious tx through as the miners hit it with a 13 block reorg. The people that lost funds were the ones using NEAR DEX which is not PoW. Again, the PoW coin is fine it's the DEX that got fucked.

62157703
>>62157402
There's nothing decentralized about "DeFi." The company behind the "DeFi" protocol (lol) is going to reimburse everyone so nobody loses anything (lol).

62157759
>>62157685
Literally nothing I said is remotely incorrect nor is anything you said remotely a correction to anything I did. I never even mentioned near dex, I just qualified this in the broader context of the structural reality of proof of work consensus and how it fails at scale, particularly in the BTC/LTC model.
>>62157703
"Cedefi" absolutely. And most "defi" also, like solana which has shown it multiple times. There are plenty of networks which are actual defi, at least as media of exchange (they aren't BTC using Lightning).

62157772
>>62157499
>>62157670
August 15, 2010 (Value Overflow Incident). BTC has had multiple reorgs but this was the largest. A bug (CVE-2010-5139) allowed a user to create 184 billion bitcoins. Lurk moar before posting. Enjoy whatever consensus change is forced to prevent quantum threat to Satoshi's wallet or the dumpening and permanent irrelevance that waits for when it is exploited.

62157819
>>62157759
I'm not going to try to convince you. Keep using your premine PoS chains desu. You deserve it.

62157911
>>62157819
You have zero argument and are coping through sophistry nodding to the concept that you are exasperated and I am missing a clear point you don't have. I hate PoS too, you retarded false dichotomy disingenuous nigger.

62157925
>>62157911
insisting that pow is equivalent to node dos is sophistry

62158205
>>62157925
No, strawmanning after being extremely specifically addressed directly and point by point is. I literally never compared the two or gave the same critique to either. You have no argument so you cope by acting like this and playing REALLY gay rhetoric games to try to signal unfavorability to the horde of consensus group thinkers hoping it will make up for it because this is how you interpret information yourself. And when faced with argument you don't know how to address you just do this one size fits all corny non-argument shit and repress it being true to cope. Really gay

62158897
>>62157772
You're ridiculous and pathetic. Everyone knows about the 184 quadrillion bitcoin overflow but this was done when the satoshi group was still working on ironing out the bugs. You say pseud shit like
>I just qualified this in the broader context of the structural reality of proof of work consensus
Why the fuck include the term "structural reality"? It adds nothing to your points and it just seems superficial and seems like the only reason its there is to fluff up your sentences to make you seem like you're smarter than you are. The reality is that you're an idiot and you cannot prove that this Litecoin zero-day was related to the consensus protocol. Go on, I'll wait. Oh what's that? You can't prove it? Yeah because it's not related to the consensus algorithm. I'll wait for you to post some more literal babbling.

62159118
>>62156949
It didn't even dip. Litecoin to $2000

62159171
>litecoin
>privacy layer
What? I'm so behind on litecoin news.
Anyway we already have good privacy coin it's Monero just use that.
Also attacks that require "spending money" aren't really code issues afaik.

62160390
>>62158897
>The thing you said happened which I didn't know about but am now pretending I did happened EXACTLY as you said it did, but it doesn't matter because now I am feigning exasperation at how YOU must be an undesirable type of person who I will now go on to do everything I can to characterize to cope/project and hope that people interpret what I am consciously knowingly doing as being vaguely like you and side with me instead of caring that you were right!
Why are you spending half your reply policing my usage of a single term? I use "structural" reality to connote the specific hard-coded, objective scaffolding rather to distinguish it from people who avoid argument like you by using the term "reality" like a rhetorical device to semantically launder subjectivity you can't sell without disingenuous word games. I literally never claimed that the bug was connected to the proof of work consensus model. I literally said this multiple times. I am giving full context of a bug being discovered (despite the litecoin group having spent YEARS working on "ironing out all the bugs" lol), its implications, and the broader aspect of proof of work and why it fails as a model. You spent your entire argument first conceding what you first ignored, pivoting to instead try to run cover for it in a way that makes it seem unmentionable or something because you can't deny something that objectively happened even if you didn't know it did. Lol You finish this with calling me a pseud for mentioning it which bridges into you next policing my language in a way that has zero to do with my argument (intentionally) and is completely avoidant with the sole purpose to make people not consider what I say and to view me as cringe in some way such that even though I am objectively correct and honestly representing things, I am somehow cringe in someway that should overrule the validity of my claims which you are now goalpost shifting/running cover for? Then you triple down on an argument

62160393
>>62158897
>>62160390
I never even made and challenge me to defend that strawman, reiterating it like 5x punctuating each one to try to make it seem like you are confidently exposing me when again I literally never even made the claim and you are just doing REALLY gay argument-avoidant sophistry because you literally know I am right or that you can't defend your argument and literally nothing could be said which would get you to break consensus signaling with your chosen CULTure because your position has nothing to do with what's true and everything to do with having been late to a big opportunity and being too unoriginal and unintelligent to and patience barren to find a new opportunity so you glom onto the squeezed lemon imagining how delicious the next round of lemonade will be. You don't care what argument makes sense or doesn't, you just care about signaling consensus with your side in narrative warfare, aiming to mask the parts that don't fit from mattering. You are indistinguishable from covid cultists/leftists and are a literal NPC. You can literally tell how mad and emotionally ejaculatory/petulant you are by the hollow and emphatically broken up loudness of your writing. It is incredibly obvious you dislike being forced to consciously live in the cognitive dissonance you accept but feel less stuffy when repressing. You literally know you are being like this and are choosing to. You know bitcoin was sold out and coopted and mutated from what Satoshi designed as a medium of exchange servicable money to a "store of value" which still aspires to be a medium of exchange but technically neutered itself to be unable to be without being the most centralized surveillance money slop imaginable using lightning etc. You guys are late normies who would sell all defi value out and live in the technate panopticon gladly for btc to be $50 M. The difference between us is you avoid argument through accusatory projection, I just describe what you did after I already argued.

62160406
>>62159171
You can use the LTC privacy layer for off ramps and on ramps. Ever swap XMR for BTC and get tainted BTC? With Litecoin you have an offramp where you don't have to worry about whether the previous owner of the coins had tainted funds or not as MWEB enhances fungibility.

62160413
>>62160393
>You guys are late normies who would sell all defi value out
You realize those of us that have been in crypto for over a decade aren't chasing the newest 'defi protocol' right? We're largely sound money enthusiasts and our core folio is fair launch PoW like XMR, BTC and LTC. It's you noobs that are chasing the shiniest object that don't understand what crypto is about

62160425
>>62157467
It all stopped when the dude who drew Lite Knights art left. 97% of you weren't here for that

62160605
>>62160390
>>62160393
You literally believe that Epstein and co. coded bitcoin and is the sole reason that bitcoin exists. It seems to me that the retarded NPC is you dumbass.

62161117
>>62160413
I have been in crypto longer than you. If you have been in crypto for over 10 years and think PoW is the end all be all state of consensus as typified in BTC, you are literally a retard or a normie outsourcing opinions to a sphere of influencers.
>>62160605
More strawmanning. I LITERALLY said they COOPTED it and steered it from Satoshi's vision of a p2p payment network medium of exchange cash alternative, which they objectively did. As of the time of revelations of Epstein's involvement leaking via email releases, At the time this letter was written, there were around 12,000 commits to Bitcoin's code. Today there are 47,583 commits to Bitcoin's code. That means that 74.79% of the Bitcoin core development and code was committed after Jeffery Epstein took over the defacto senior management role as benefactor. He may not have been 'Satoshi', but he was absolutely running the executive direction of bitcoin on behalf of Mossad for the majority of bitcoin's development, and crucially, at the time the decisions were made to neuter it as money to convert it into a store of value nonsense that requires centralization to scale to be even remotely usable. Bitcoin (and ethgate ethereum) was ALWAYS about making it unusable outside turbo-centralized L2s basically promising defi L1 principles that forcibly funnel into the trojan horse payload of centralized business solutions L2 programmable surveillance money Hell. They tried (and failed) to regulate everything else and give a regulatory free pass to Eth and BTC, but the tech is SO much slower and more expensive and inelegant and vulnerable even the 6 year moat of complete regulatory capture was inadequate to seal the deal and they are hemorrhaging share. Clarity will seal the deal. You can like it or not like it, it's gonna happen. More efficient, better, quantum safe tech will run because bitcoin crawled.

62161133
>>62160605
>>62161117
Forgot pic related. And by "today" I meant to as of the release of the letter. I copied from an old post of mine in the archives and forgot to clarify that.

62161145
>>62161117
>PoW is le bad
Monero is the most legitimate coin and it's PoW. But please tell me more about your 'defi investments', moonboy.

62161189
>>62157759
how do i know which smart contract platforms are "true defi"? i want to use chain that are censorproof. can solana blacklist wallets and roll back transactions?

62161471
>>62156949
How about we build more node implementations for Litecoin AND BITCOIN so single codebase (Core) issues like this won't happen? Anyone? No? Okay.

62164480
>>62161145
>>62161189
I have tried to post picrel 5 times on multiple networks and devices and it submits but does not post for some reason.

62165827
>>62161117
so... use litecoin instead? bch?

62165936
>>62164480
>get "51% attacked"
>actually its closer to 30% but maths r hard n stuff
>ends up just costing the attacker tons of money to keep the "attack" up
>ends up simply reinforcing current security paradigm
Xmr definitely needs more minors and chodes but I'd say its bretty gud where its at for the moment

item.title: >tfw I realized yesterday I could have unironically retired if I had just sold my bitshit in October >tfw I want to kms… — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw I realized yesterday I could have unironically retired if I had just sold my bitshit in October >tfw I want to kms…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-28 17:13 UTC

OP

>tfw I realized yesterday I could have unironically retired if I had just sold my bitshit in October
>tfw I want to kms everyday because I'm sick of waging
>tfw I have to wait for this gay retarded nigger market to go back up to escape the wagie cagie
Goddamn I'm so stupid falling for the hodl meme. I'm ngmi, it's OVER.

Early replies (first 3)

62163272
It will drop further then crab for awhile, you will give up and sell and a few weeks after it will skyrocket to the new ATH.

62163282
>>62163272
I know retard, I already said it's OVER. 6 years of hodling for NOTHING but max pain and agony in this peepeepoopoo ana rape market. I unironically hold ROSE, the prolapsed anus coin, btw.

62163438
>>62163282
You’re gonna be waiting a long fuckin time

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62163472
>>62163268
Dont underestimate the power of positive thinking. Just 3 more years

62163476
>>62163268
don't lose hope top g u got this

62163498
>>62163268
Nigga I could have made 9k from 2k in 2017. I could have made 20k from 3k in 2021. I could have made 500k from 5k in 2025. Nigga you feel me you nome sayin nigga?

62166193
>>62163472
>Just 3 more years

item.title: If you missed: - The golden FAANG era with $500k total comp salaries - The early crypto bullruns Then it is over. — 9 replies — 18.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you missed: - The golden FAANG era with $500k total comp salaries - The early crypto bullruns Then it is over.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:44 UTC

OP

If you missed:

- The golden FAANG era with $500k total comp salaries
- The early crypto bullruns

Then it is over. You are now part of the permanent underclass.

Early replies (first 3)

62159500
>>62159486
If you missed

- The buildup to the DOTCOM bubble
- Vietnam war racketeering
- The Black Hills gold rush
- Being born into European aristocracy

Then it is over. You are now, as you've ever been, part of the permanent underclass.

62159514
>>62159486
>saaar buy my bags
Naaah.

62159674
>>62159486
If you are online all the time (/biz/) and even care about being in western civiligoydem then you are apart of the terminally online underclass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62159822
>>62159674
>western civiligoydem

62162043
You WILL pay the token tax

62165845
>>62159486
Well you've also missed all the high paying AI research jobs

62165862
>>62159486
let me guess you sidelined yourself for the last year and now coping by saying we're all screwed. By the way those 500k comp swe guys were extreme outliers and there's tons of doctors and lawyers making that much anyways

62166003
>>62159486
I am in the awkward position of having 430k in crypto and only needing a 3x and a couple shitty 190k condos and about 120k in commodities ETF. I feel almost like I still have a chance at makimg it but also at same time feel like I failed any the easy gains and only shot is gambling now to get any more gains. I still live with my mom….

62166088
>>62159486
BBBottom signal

item.title: LINK will hit $250 before the year ends. — 4 replies — 16.0/hr

LINK will hit $250 before the year ends.

OP

LINK will hit $250 before the year ends.

I can feel it in my bones.

62166105
250 pesos maybe

62166175

more like 250 yen lmao

62166181

This is not fucking funny. This is MY LIFE

item.title: IT'S BEGINNING — 3 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT'S BEGINNING

FUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCKFUCK

62166092

62166139

item.title: What's a better plan with $150k? A) All in MSTR this Oct. Dca every paycheck for 2-3 years. Sell after a 5-10x profit. B… — 2 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's a better plan with $150k? A) All in MSTR this Oct. Dca every paycheck for 2-3 years. Sell after a 5-10x profit. B…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:56 UTC

OP

What's a better plan with $150k?

A) All in MSTR this Oct. Dca every paycheck for 2-3 years. Sell after a 5-10x profit.

B) 1/3 SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI once they IPO. Dca every paycheck for 10 years. Sell after a 5-10x profit.

Early replies (first 3)

62166023
>>62166018
probably find the next tesla and anthropic and invest in those

62166130
>>62166023
good idea

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: >tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this? — 93 replies — 54.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this?

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-04-28 15:31 UTC

OP

>tfw fell for the NEET meme
how the fuck can you guys endure this? it's so boring and depressing, I unironically miss my wagecuck 9-5

Early replies (first 3)

62156768
I would unironically wagecuck still if I was able to take vacation whenever I want. I don't enjoyed scheduling things like that or requiring permission.

62156775
>>62156751
I became a neet because of crippling social anxiety, not out of choice.
I suspect many are the same.

62156779
>>62156751
see you fell for it without having any money, I did that once and then realized that it costs money to do anything other than sit in front of a screen all day every day

Depending on where you live, even going out for a walk will cost you bus fare or gas to get to a decent park. Never felt so trapped, now if I had money to go out and do things like camping or fishing or even just cook nice meals then I would have been a lot happier

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62159386
>>62159375
You will stop working when you make 1m+ a year from doing nothing

62159410
>>62159386
Who the fuck makes that kind of money? Besides, there is plenty of already rich doctors. Maybe i would asses again exactly what i want to do for work in that situation.

62159425
>>62159410
> Who the fuck makes that kind of money
Everyone who grew up above middle class.
> Besides, there is plenty of already rich doctors.
Exactly. So these doctors bring in over 1m a year in passive income and still work as a doctor?

62159435
>>62159375
>>62159410
So, just like everyone who says “i love my job,” it is only because you have to work it. The moment you don’t get paid for it, you don’t love it and wouldn’t do it for free.

62159530
>>62156751
yeah its horrible i wish i could get any job but i cant even get helldesk with bachelors and experience

hopefully this cert im studying for will land me some job but we will see

62159557
If my living conditions remain the same no matter how much I work, and taxes take all my extra work, what is legitimately the point of working?

62159586
>>62157072
Lololol

62159864
>>62156751
most people are NEET incorrectly, living like bums or mooching off their parents when you SHOULD be mooching off the government
Go get on welfare. Go get on food assistance. Go get on whatever you can and get free gibs from the government.

I get almost $300 a month in foodstamps and $450 in cash assistance. My rent is subsidized based on my income so I pay pennies. I apply for energy assistance programs to pay my light bill for over half the year. I never have to work and all I do is sit around all day long, watch youtube, listen to music, read manga, play video games, draw, and lurk.

62159895
>>62159864
Mooching off parents mean building wealth.

62160013
>>62159864
i don't think i even qualify

62162244
>>62159435
Of course i wouldn’t do it for free but i don’t work for the best possible pay either. There is s balance to it, and it skews heavily on meaningfulness of work instead of the pay.

62162251
>>62159425
This is just weak.

62162273
>>62159864
I noticed you wrote light bill and bragged about being on welfare. please post your hand.

62163096
>>62156775
Take trintellix 10mg it literally cured me I used to have real bad social anxiety and always wanting to fight with people going outside now I just feel chill and normal not stuck in my own head 24/7.


It will make your dick not work though

62163114
>>62156751
Working from home is boring as well, but at least I have money.

62163252
>>62156751
i hate working as much as being a neet
live is shit eitherway when poor and lonely
i just want to go back to school time

62163885
>>62156751
>it's so boring and depressing
Why aren't you doing stuff you like to do with your time? This shitty complaint is always a (You) problem that only the most boring, mindless cattle have.

62163908
>>62156751
A lot of the NEET propaganda is from autists who essentially can't function in normal circumstances and cope by seething about it. I feel for them, but they have to know it's retarded.
Mankind is built for working towards something. Even if you're rich, you can end up like Citzen Kane.

62164059
We can't all make it so shut up.

62164079
Creating need space. Like, I dunno, woodwork or building furniture or whatever.
You can't do that if you don't have a house. Living the NEETlife in a flat, even if a nice flat, is incredibly limiting. Not having a house limit the potential hobbies.

62164110
>>62156751
the NEET life is a perfect mirror.
if you are depressed and bored as a NEET, you are simply meant to be a slave.
There is nothing else to say.

62164118
>>62156795
this is the move, anon.
im an electrical engineering student and my plan is to take my intern cash and start a biz while working remote somewhere.
good luck

62164142
>>62156838
Let me guess, your hobbies are going on 4chan and masturbating

62164460
>NEETs when you ask them to show you some of the art and projects they "have time to create since they're not a slave like you"

62164470
>>62164460
i've beaten my favorite game nearly 5 times in the past month and wrote some genderbent DEI thot getting raped and mindbroken
how 'bout u?

62164922
>>62156751
simple i wasn't born a slave with a pink id

62165197
>>62164460
Is it so difficult to grasp why somebody who isn't compelled to conform to working their whole life is also not compelled to try and impress somebody who is?

62165794
>>62156775
yeah i never had a job because i keep sperging out in the few interviews i get

62165803
>>62164460
I just feel bad for their Mom's more than anything.

62165810
>>62157256
AI is based and does the stupid shit I don't want to do
sucks for the accountants that get left out, great for the ones that survive. Bookkeepers / entry level on suicide watch.

item.title: UAE quits OPEC — 14 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
UAE quits OPEC

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:43 UTC

OP

Can one of our in house oil investors explain the consequences of such a move and share the calculations and graphs they made in advance for such an event unfolding to justify their original investment.

Early replies (first 3)

62165070
I don't know shit but if they're quitting OPEC it must be because they want to produce more than OPEC agreed to. They could always produce less, OPEC wouldn't mind. So bearish for oil, a little bit?
Or am I retarded

62165079
nothingburger

62165082
>>62165059
it's so over

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62165096
>>62165070
UAE currently needs to or rather even can export more oil?
But long term definitely bearish unless this is seen as greater instability in the gulf region.

62165408
>>62165059
Short term it changes nothing.

62165597
>>62165059
>Since the 1980s, OPEC has had a limited impact on world oil-supply and oil-price stability, as there is frequent cheating by members on their commitments to one another
KEK
non-white people really are a plague aren't they?

62165715
>>62165597
I would like to figure out

62165721
>>62165597
Yes

62165724
>>62165597
>non-white people really are a plague aren't they?
Says the white anglo jew who behaves like a pirate thug.

62165763
>>62165597
Every OPEC meeting is exactly the fucking same
>"Okay everybody, there's too much oil supply and prices are too low, we all agree to cut to raise the price of oil, right?”
>"Of course!"
>*Everyone goes back home*
>"Those dipshits fell for it hook line and sinker, now we can raise production while everyone else cuts there's"
>All OPEC countries raise production
>Rinse and repeat forever

62165809
>>62165724
White Anglo Saxon men are talking. Be quiet and listen

62165957
>>62165059
are they even producing or exporting anything after the attacks from iran?

62166011
>>62165724
Hello jamboy

62166086
what's with amerimutts always pretending to be "whilte" and victims?

item.title: boy am i glad i didnt fall for the blue collar meme >20 days vacation >10 sick days >11 public holidays >wfh 2 days a we… — 28 replies — 26.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
boy am i glad i didnt fall for the blue collar meme >20 days vacation >10 sick days >11 public holidays >wfh 2 days a we…

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:09 UTC

OP

boy am i glad i didnt fall for the blue collar meme

>20 days vacation
>10 sick days
>11 public holidays
>wfh 2 days a week
>40 hours a week MAX

who here /comfy wagie/

Early replies (first 3)

62159605
>>62159597
That doesn't mean anything to the Wagie, they do it for the humiliation of it all not the money or benefits.

62159615
>>62159597
>>62159605
Laborers fuck up their body's, are always tired after work, and get fat after 30. You're also immediately known to be low class

62159618
>>62159615
these plebs survive on gas station goyslop

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62159632
>>62159597
give me ur job please i want a nice do nothing six fig white collar job

but instead im a cursed NEET

62159638
>>62159618
Anon, you're telling me, energy drinks, Big AZ burgers and smoking/vaping makes you look like shit?

62159649
>>62159597
Remote work too if your company doesn't suck. I love my comfy office job. For some reason, blue collars love to brag how much overtime they work, as if it's something to be proud of. And yet some of them still seem poor.

62159662
>>62159649
blue collar is only recommended if you are an iq-let

62159679
>>62159597
We don't want you in the trades either. Your thin skinned ass wouldn't "get" the manly jokes and pranks we do while doing real work.

62159687
>>62159618
>>62159638

62159730
>>62159597
>>62159679
we don’t want you in the trades either. you’d be running to hr the first time someone jokes around in the warehouse, you wouldn’t last a day around it this aint disneyland, princess. we joke around hard out on the warehouse floor haha

62159794
>>62159615
>>62159679
>>62159730
Not working anything but jews hat blue collar workers. Even a certain german politician would notice this

62159985
thank you for your service

62163583
>>62159597
6 months neet here

62163588
>>62159597
$150k, fully remote, 22 days PTO + 11 company holidays, half day fridays from memorial day to labor day.

62163622
>>62159679
kek tradies have to plow each other because all the females are in white collar office spaces

62163863
>>62159597
I love how many men fell for the trades meme during the Biden years because some Boomer/grifter/Boomer grifter who doesn't even work a trade told them it was good money only to realize how fucking bad they are. That "good money" is maybe $85k that you'll make ONLY if you're working large amount of OT.
>earn while you learn!
Nobody teaches you shit and your "learning" experiences come in the form of last second requests to do something you have no idea about

>t. Worked a trade for 4.5 years, quit, and got an analyst job.

62163932
>>62163863
how did you switch careers?

62163966
>>62159615
Sorta. If you don't drink like a nigger and eat decent you stay way healthier than the fat fuck desk workers. And your dick works.

62163975
>>62163932
He has never actually worked a trade

62164000
>>62163932
I quit one career and went to another. Granted, this was before this current nigger job market so I supposed luck played into it a bit.

>>62163975
Except I did?

62164931
I’m on my 15th year of doing commercial hvac. Were union, so the pay and benefits are good. We get to take our vans home and we have a company phone, all paid for. Dynamic work environment. Work alone most of the time. Micromanaging almost non existent. Flexible work schedule. Just name some of the pros.

Sure there are cons to it as well like anything else but some of those are non issues (you might sweat from time to time…). Guys who have been here as long or longer than me aren’t using walkers to get around. We’re all perfectly fit. There are tools for every job. The ‘broken body’ issue is a meme from people who never worked in the field or are hurting themselves. That goes for pipefitters, construction workers, etc. companies get away with shitty work environments because workers put up with it. I hate waging, period, but it’s the best thing I found so far. The sooner I can stop the better though

62165179
>>62159597
True words.

Anyone who's ever worked blue collar job understands that the real money is in operating a business (roofing, plumbing company, etc), not in the labor itself. Being self-employed blue collar or even employed blue collar will always keep you in the low income category or middle income at best.

It is just a psyop to further push down the cost of blue collar labor since unlike white collar work, it is hard to automate with AI. The value of physical, blue-collar work will be pushed down further as many people ditch white collar and try to move into blue-collar jobs due to AI automation, further pushing down the value of physical blue-collar labor.

Let the blue-collar goyim deal in the real world.

62165334
>>62164000
>Except I did?
You really didn't anon. Let's be honest

62165619
>>62159597
Why do you have so few vacation days?

>32 days vacation
>11 public holidays
>if a public holiday falls on a weekend, you get another day of vacation to choose freely
>WFH 20 hours per week
>40 hours a week, flexible schedule

62165627
>>62159597
>>62165619
Also what the hell is a sick day? You're sick, you stay home. What else are you supposed to do? Doctor's note if it's more than 3 days obviously

62165628
>>62159632
How?Just get a warehouse job, work a few years and then get a forklift cert
Easy 50 - 60k a year

62165640
>>62159649
they give all their $$ to their wives so it all disappears forever

62165955
>>62159597
Not longer than two years I swear.

item.title: TSLA quarterly profit — 16 replies — 18.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
TSLA quarterly profit

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:22 UTC

OP

Is Tesla about to be the first big growth company sliding back to being unprofitable?
What is their way out?

>https://www.macrotrends.net/stocks/charts/TSLA/tesla/net-income

Early replies (first 3)

62162749
>>62162712
>quarterly profit
Their capex guidance for 2026 is $25 Billions.
>What is their way out?
Fsd subscription, cyber cab, batteries/energy storage, robots, Digital Optimus.

62162815
i will never buy tesla
for me its the ethereum of stocks
only onion faggots buy eth and tsla

62162837
>>62162712
You just don't get it the market is forward looking and they see the writing on the wall with all the political networking being done on the regulatory side. There is clearly a large government contract in the pipeline for robotics where the president will hire a TSLA robot to infiltrate fort Knox and steal all the gold but then in an unforeseen twist that gold will of course be gone and they will try to pin it on the robot which will become a fugitive, backstabbed, betrayed and on the run, he will then don an eye patch and start his own PMC to clear his name of any wrongdoing and only at that moment will everyone realize that robots can actually develop consciousness. And then you'll be sorry you didn't invest in this incredible growth story.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62162903
>>62162712
Tsla sucks elon is gay wish I had bought TSLL at this time in 2024

62162959
>>62162749
>Subscriptions for services in your car
I feel like this is going to blow up in their faces.

62162978
>>62162712
Growth means low profit retard. They are reinvesting their revenue into new technology, not giving it back to investors who want a safe return.

62163285
>>62162978
They're paying off HUGE debt for buying Twitter at double price in the past.

62163439
>>62162712
I wouldn't pay for FSD sub. Idk why they think people would. I have a 2020 model 3 and bought the full license, which is nice. I use it all the time. But no one is gonna pay $100 per month subscription to have it. Maybe if the autonomous cab thing catches on people would pay. But the cost is too high

62164697
>>62162959
$100 is probably a bit steep for most. At a more reasonable price it would be worth it if you can sleep, work, watch videos or whatever instead of being stuck in traffic or have long commutes for hours every day.
That could also be something for companies to offer as a benefit for employees.

62165662
>>62162712
i hate the tesla cult
if you buy tsla you deserve a hernia

62165690
"people" rather fomo into TSLA at ATH instead of buying LINK

This clown world is too much

62165726
>>62162712
Elon has already explicitly stated that without AGI it's over (it's over).

62165791
>>62162712
didn't they have spacex buy a couple thousand cybertrucks recently? they are desperate...

62165823
>>62165791
They sold 1.65 million cars last year.

62165843
>>62165726
Elon is a dumbass on drugs and he likes lying a lot.

62165998
>>62165823
only because chinese EV companies are forbidden from selling in the US

item.title: I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS???? — 18 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS????

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-28 16:01 UTC

OP

I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS????

Early replies (first 3)

62165377
>>62165360
Become an overnight security guard with a site in a rich area that’s internal (you’re inside a building).

The golden goose is MAG7 tech companies. I’ve literally and unironically done security for google. Max comfy

62165379
>>62165360
I do. I work like ~5 hours a day and play on my phone while eating a ton of corporate junk food and hitting on the co-workers for the rest. Must be a skill issue.

62165445
>>62165360
E Z ly
U just week

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62165491
>>62165360
More like 45+ years.

62165614
>>62165360
Waging used to get you enough money to buy a house in a White area, get a foid and still have enough money left over to save. Now waging doesn't provide you enough to do any of that, so its pointless.

62165647
I've had it free seating in open landscapes, I'm switching from 90% office presence to 90% wfh.

62165675
>>62165360
When you become an adult and have to wage, you either become a soulless wagie husk, kill yourself, or become a NEET. That's what they don't tell you growing up. Life is a scam.

62165695
>>62165360
there's only 1 thing worse than being a wagie, and that's applying to be a wagie and being rejected

62165737
when I was young I'd get excited over changing the world with my ideas and becoming a big shot at my company
now I just wanna get out and retire ASAP
where did it all go wrong
I wonder how many of us are like this

62165765
>>62165360
>ugh i hate swimming how do people do this for x years instead of just drowning
you will look back at this point in time as relative golden age, affordable food, relative high trust, available work as a means to sustain yourself. you have no idea how bad its going to get.

62165773
>>62165360
I hate waging so much, it only becomes bearable when I take adderall and zap my brain, rendering me a soulless NPC. Rotting away in my little wage cage. Unfortunately (or maybe fortunately) this is not sustainable for 30+ years.

62165800
>>62165773
>>62165647
Oh yeah and you 100% need to secure a job that is at least 1 day minimum WFH per week (2 to further keep your sanity). I'd actually kill myself if I had to be in the office at 8am 5 days a week, that's not a life worth living.

62165806
>>62165737
>where did it all go wrong
when you realized that most "people" are not

62165808
>>62165360
The key is to lose hope. Once you lose hope it's easier to live in hell. That's a lesson I've learned from my waging years

62165811
>>62165360
I got rich on my own and then went to a government job for fun, prestige. Agreed.

Especially if you're not making bookoobucks it'd be soul crushingly hellish to earn baby money in some bleh position, a really significant amount that goes to paying for transportation, food and clothes specific to the office.

It's especially wild for women to do this shit in Jewish daycare when they could just stay home and between the not-commuting, the better home make meals

62165817
>>62165360
WFH at least hybrid solutions or fully remote, 40hr contracts MAX and 35hr ideal, im using 20hr+ a week to put into my own businesses and side hustles to supplement my income and eventually if fortunate enough replace my job.

62165830
>>62165377
What is the pay and bennies like? Wouldn't all the tech companies just automate security?

62165918
>>62165811
Women enjoy keeping society running.

item.title: >as soon as staking arrives, its over for you hoes >as soon as CCIP arrives, its over for you hoes >as soon as staking V… — 7 replies — 14.0/hr

OP

>as soon as staking arrives, its over for you hoes
>as soon as CCIP arrives, its over for you hoes
>as soon as staking V2 arrives, its over for you hoes
>as soon as Sergey stops farting in the dev room, it's over for you hoes

>as soon as SmartCon 2023,2025,2026 happens, it's over for you hoes
>as soon as Chainlink reveals all of its bank partnerships, its over for you hoes
>as soon as Chainlink reveals its Swift partnership, it's over for you hoes
>*you are here*
>as soon as Clarity act is passed it's over for you hoes

When are you going to face the music anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62165492
When pajeets stop getting paid to FUD link

62165537
hating stinks. it's simply what we do

62165544
>>62165480
not selling tho

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62165579
Chainlink is LITERALLY a cuckold club, they brag about all the money they lost over the past 6 years

62165755
>>62165480
Who are you even fudding to? Theres like 3 people left here and none of them are selling lol

62166064
>>62165480
Reminder adem was right. He won

62166087
>>62165755
if we get even 1 newcomer here to eschew link, we can die happy knowing our lives were not in vain

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 50 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-04-28 10:33 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146544
>>62146532
>paid marketeer
I'm not the one lying, moving goalposts, forgetting my own talking points, and changing IDs.

Go ahead, name one CL product that does not use CL nodes (and therefore the Link token).

62146565
>>62146506
kill yourself before I find you, Rajesh

62146578
>says some bullshit
>gets debunked
>changes IDs and gets extremely emotional

many such cases, sad!

62147333
>>62145068
>to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community?
What ecosystem? The one they claim is coming? None of the companies they bought have integrated XRPL.

62147341
>>62143203
>dont come to /biz/ for a year
>its still the same fucking threads by cultist linkies and xrp schizos
We’ll see you next year

62147491
>>62147341
it's literally the same 4 retards in every fucking thread

62147758
>>62146458
literally the same can be said of xrp kek

62147964
Chainlink is such a pathetic little project...

62148311
>>62147341
next year chainlink will be dead and buried though, and the catalogue will be flooded with frogposters crying about "why didn't you tell me????"

62151224
>>62147758
Fucking how?

62154431
>>62146230
Ripple don't sell to retail and their products are building around actual use. Chainlink sell every green candle directly to retail and built zero utility for the token that is itself completely disconnected from the actual product.

62155143
>>62154431
>Ripple don't sell to retail
After the lawsuit and the SEC waiver, if Ripple wanted to sell to institutions they'd have to file an application with the SEC saying "we want to sell securities".

These applications are public, so anyone can verify that Ripple have never filed such an application to sell XRP to institutions: https://www.sec.gov/edgar/browse/?CIK=1685012
Thus they've been selling to retail exclusively.

>built zero utility for the token
See >>62146402
Link token utility is absolute; every single CL feature and product requires it.

Meanwhile Ripple pretty much only builds products and buys companies that have nothing inherently to do with XRP.

62155954
i made this thread
you are all spiritually indian
when indians get criticized they say “well pakistan is shittier!”. i dont care about chainlink, i dont invest in link, its probably a paypig investor scam too, i made this thread about xrp and you xrp schizos all had to create this charicture in ur mind about how i must be a link shill

no
of ur first thought after reading a criticism of xrp is “well at least we arent chainlink investors” then,
you are spiritually indian

62156263
>>62155143
None of you have ever been able to explain how that would cause the price of the fundraising token to increase. If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure? I’m also slightly retarded, so feel free to explain it to me

62156366
>>62156263
>If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure?
That's how blockchain miners have always operated, you dip.

62156399
>>62156366
oh so it's not the future

62156865
>>62156399
Blockchain is absolutely the future. And it needs external data to become it.

62158109
>>62143238
Land

62158769
>>62147333
Checked but a 20 second search tells me you're incorrect.

62158900
>>62158769
Yet you didnt post any.

62158933
>>62143238
i hate brad so much

62158942
>>62156865
sure but chainst1nk is dinosaur tech
it's going to be the pets.com of blockchain

62158980
>>62158900
I'll save you the 20 seconds.
Ripple Treasury is an enterprise treasury management system (TMS) launched by Ripple following its $1 billion acquisition of the legacy treasury software provider GTreasury in October 2025.
Tell your boss to update the script.

62160355
>>62143203
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62161098
>>62158980
That shit works exactly the same as before Ripple bought it; without needing XRP or XRPL.

62161538
>>62158980
You wasted 20 seconds. That doesn't use XRPL. Its literally an existing business that has better cash flows than anything ripple has ever created.

62161619
>>62161538
>>62161098
My mistake i read it as XRP not XRPL. Lucky I'm at work and got paid for the wasted 20 seconds.

62161662
>>62161619
It uses neither.

62164478
>>62161662
kek, XRPaypigs BTFO

62164747
>>62143203
obviously. only the market has the power to crush those endless waves of cucks and morons posting about their bags 24/24.

item.title: Is 300k at 30 y/o enough to NEET retire? — 26 replies — 5.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is 300k at 30 y/o enough to NEET retire?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-28 12:25 UTC

OP

>24 years old warehouse wagie
>go to work
>go to gym after (hit 2 plate bench milestone a long while ago, lean af, get mired)
>come home, cook, meditate, read, sleep
>minimalist and don't really spend a dime on shit, live with parents rent-free
>no friends, no online friends, no girlfriend, no masturbation, no socializing, no unnecessary spending, no parties, no drugs/alcohol, no fashion, no fastfood/junk, nothing
>have 130k saved up so far, 100k of that invested
So I'm planning to stack up every single penny of my paycheck and I reckon I'd have 300k-500k by 30 years old. I kinda just wanna NEET and chill at my moms basement while my money works for me in the background, then in 10 years time when the 300k becomes a mill I can fully retire with autonomy. I plan on continuing mastering myself without having to adroitly balance a full-time job. I've also transcended most normans in mental and physical acuity so it's quite literally impossible to interact with them in any meaningful capacity; my fulfillment is independent of their proximity. I'm already at the point of my spirituality where indulging in lust has serious palpable physical drawbacks and lethargy (and it's not placebo, I've experimented). So even when I retire there won't be any exorbitant expenses attributed to marriage, kids, travelling, etc.. Also gonna shave my head so no barber. Aside from mastering my ninjutsu when I NEET, as a pass time I'll stick to old vidya / ROM hacks for mastery purposes, so no splurge on ultra normie triple A titles and new consoles/PCs. Can I pull it off?

Early replies (first 3)

62162036
Yes with your lifesytle easily. But that lifestyle is better in SEA, we are tropical animals.

62162053
you need to know what your yearly costs are, expected annual returns, then factor in the odds of some negative years. in general you need a lot more money than a few hundred thousand to be able to live off of. with as young as you are, you wouldn’t really want to spend the principal.

62162057
>>62162013
Take the south/central America pill over the SEAmonkey pill

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62162076
>>62162013
150k at 25 is fucking insane for your age
I'm 25 and I barely have 15k NW

62162082
>>62162013
Anyone in here that tells you this is a nice life hasn't made it. It's hell on earth depressing and stressful as fuck neeting on low 6 figs. Grind out to 700k+ and then relax, I almost killed myself neeting with only like 400k in the bank cash

62162102
>>62162013
if you invest it right you can NEET at 35-40

62162107
>>62162013
You sound autistic as hell

62162108
>work in warehouse
>anime
>virgin who's never known love
>mentally transending anything
You've got a lot to learn lil buddy. 1 mil in 10 years won't buy you much

62162208
>>62162082
Even 700k isn't enough though pretty close. You either keep it in cash and lose purchasing power or keep it in risk assets and risk the bubble bursting.
Going half half is the worst of both worlds with such an amount

62162214
nice blog

62162224
>>62162082
>>62162208
>>62162053
I'm sure some kinda shitty kinda not-so-shitty studio apartment can be comfortably rented out off the dividends from a 700k+ portfolio.. right? And even worst case scenario I run out of dough, by the age I do I'd probably qualify for some government gibs

62162380
>>62162013
I have 300k at 29, and it feels like nothing. Do I just buy a 600k house or something?

62163303
>>62162013
if you save in bitcoin I'd say you have a chance to make it

62163883
>>62162013
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62163915
>>62162013
Sure, just go all in on a leveraged AI-bubble ETF with ALL that money and hold it until the bubble actually pops. MU probably still has growth, but SNDK has had the most growth of all. Get into SNXX. SNDU closed slightly higher than 2x, but I don't know why. Meanwhile, panic dips will generally resolve themselves within a month.
Kishi's Sasuke wank was bullshit, btw. You are not cool or edgy for avatarfagging with retard like that. 'Oh! What's that? You collected the eyes of almost the entirety of my dead clan? I guess I should trust you!'

62163916
> no friends, no online friends, no girlfriend, no masturbation, no socializing, no unnecessary spending, no parties, no drugs/alcohol, no fashion, no fastfood/junk, nothing

Wtf monk mode, i hate monk and begging with moral teachings

62163953
>>62162013
You can neet with 300k but you'll have to move to eastern Europe, or South East Asia

62164001
>>62163953
Where in EE can you retire with 300k? You would need to live like a minimum wage local, and you certainly don't want that.
Even in SEA 300k is not enough, considering the skin tax

62164077
300k in the west is fuck all
You can probably live for 5-10 years with our inflation rate

You can probably manage a frugal lifestyle in SEA or south Europe however

62164083
>>62162013
I think so provided inflation doesn't go too high and your investments generate a good return. 400k is my goal for living on my own. If I can reach that, then I can neet passively, though I'd still actively trade to attain more wealth. Right now 200k is my goal as that should likely calm my parent's from worrying about me being a neet in their house. I should be able to invest/gamble up to 400k in such a situation.

62164104
Those are rookie numbers. You need 3 million to retire at 30.

62164670
>>62162013
why don't you just reduce your hours at work instead of going full neet? just half your hours and see how you feel.

62164709
>>62164001
What I mean is you still have to let that 300k work for you, find some kind of investment to grow it. But you can do this more comfortably in a cheap country and you'll have to take less risks.

62164935
>>62162013
>in 10 years time when the 300k becomes a mill
Why wait for that when for a cumulative few days of your life you could do the research needed to 10x this? You don't even need to go all in with 300k. I've found dips/low price stuff that turned around with enough force to turn 1-5K into millions. Did not even do grade 12 math. Just keep an eye out for opportunity.

62164971
>>62162076
It depends on the inflation and value of your NW. If you're not from America, assets are usually valued at 50% less. So if you have 3 houses in South Asia or Latin America, that's just around 60k NW. In America, that would be around 150k NW.

62165000
>>62164971
I made a mistake. That would be 60k EACH and 150k Each, not in three houses.
